title: Wiki + now over MCP: hunch_now and hunch_wiki_status, public-store by construction
context: Shipped from the roadmap. The wiki and the hot view were CLI-only — invisible to assistants that don't shell out.
decision: Two read-only MCP tools in the client-agnostic server: hunch_now (recent decisions + human-vouched roadmap + pending-review count — mirrors nowData; call at session start or before planning) and hunch_wiki_status (per-page freshness incl. specs/now/index/adoptions + doc grade counts — mirrors wikiStatus; call before trusting wiki pages). Both read the PUBLIC store only: an assistant may paste tool output anywhere, so it must be publishable by construction (dec_9c4289a4bb doctrine); the union view stays behind `hunch now --private` on the local terminal.
alternatives_rejected: Union reads with a privacy flag: a flag is one forgotten default away from a leak; jurisdiction by construction, as everywhere else in the wiki
A single combined status tool: orientation (now) and freshness (wiki) are different moments — the moment-grouped grounding list needs them separable
